title: FLF: BITS Exfiltration Hunt
description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for BITS abuse through bitsadmin.exe or Start-BitsTransfer commands that reference external HTTP/HTTPS destinations or suspicious file-transfer behavior.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs') and ('Process Name' like '*\\bitsadmin.exe' or 'Command Line' like '*Start-BitsTransfer*' or 'Command Line' like '*bitsadmin*') and ('Command Line' like '*http*' or 'Destination IP' != null or 'Destination Hostname' != null) | stats count as Events, unique('Command Line') as Commands, unique('Destination IP') as Destinations, unique('Destination Hostname') as Hostnames by 'Host Name (Server)', 'Process Name' | sort -Events

ask_ai_prompts: Which BITS commands or network events look like exfiltration rather than update traffic?
Identify the host, command line, and destination that should be investigated first.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - BITS As Exfil

falsepositives: Windows Update or enterprise software distribution
